chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالمجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/277)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل كالمجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/277)
text: رِوَايَةِ سَعْدَانَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مِثْلَهُ إِلَّا أَنَّهُ قَالَ أَهْوَنُهُ الْجُنُونُ وَ الْجُذَامُ وَ الْبَرَصُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ شَقِيّاً رَجَوْتُ أَنْ يُحَوِّلَهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ إِلَى السَّعَادَةِ .

en.hubeali: And in a report of Sa’dan from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, similar to it, except that he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The easiest of it being the insanity, and the leprosy, and the vitiligo. And if he was a wretch, it would be imminent that Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Transform him to be the fortunate one’.
en.sarwar: 26. In the Hadith from Su’dan from abu Basir from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) a similar Hadith is narrated except this: “. . . the least of which is leprosy and insanity and if he is listed among the unfortunate ones I can hope that Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy, will change him to fortunate ones.”
